title: sweat / sweatbee
body:
# sweat / sweatbee

Bodily-heat register that gets its own release name (**SWEATBEE**, plus
the **SWEAT BEE: DANCE MIX** variant) and a few scattered track-level
appearances.

## Releases

- **SWEATBEE** (2012) — five tracks numbered i–v in roman numerals (a
  rare formal-numbering choice in Colin's catalog; most releases use
  word-titles).
- **SWEAT BEE: DANCE MIX** (vault: `[[releases/alphabets-sweat-bee-dance-mix]]`)
- The SWEATBEE folder also exists under `18 alphabets/SWEATBEE/` —
  duplicate or alternative-mix copy.

## Track-title appearances elsewhere

- `may10/sweat.mp3`
- "Cave Rave" → "sets a sweaty stage on the path to Fantasia" _(per Westword
  preview headline; the word also appears in adjacent press)_

## Reading

Where the **sun** motif is bright/social and the **howl** motif is
nocturnal/dream-coded, **sweat** is the rave/dance register made bodily —
heat from movement and crowd. The "BEE" coinage adds a buzz/swarm
character; the "SWEAT BEE: DANCE MIX" framing makes the dance-floor
context explicit. Closely related to Colin's broader rave/dance-floor
vocabulary, which connects to **Fantasia** (the Rhinoceropolis festival
he co-curated) and **CAVE RAVE** (the 2014 album).

The roman-numeraled SWEATBEE tracks (i–v) suggest Colin sometimes treated
the motif formally — these aren't "sweatbee 1, 2, 3" but a discrete
five-piece work.

## Notes for future passes

- Cross-check the duplicate SWEATBEE folders to determine if the `18
  alphabets/SWEATBEE/` instance differs from the canonical 2012 version.
- The SWEAT BEE: DANCE MIX is in the vault but its relationship to
  SWEATBEE 2012 is worth clarifying — remix vs. live mix vs. alt edit.
